commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Weather emojis lack ARIA labels or role='img', making the primary visual indicator of weather status inaccessible to screen readers.
issues.fix: Wrap emojis in a span with role='img' and an aria-label describing the condition.

issues.description: Wind speed unit is hardcoded to 'km/h', which might conflict if the 'unit' prop is set to Imperial (F).
issues.fix: Determine wind speed unit based on the temperature unit prop (e.g., mph for F, km/h for C).

issues.description: Using new Date() inside the component body can cause hydration mismatches in SSR environments.
issues.fix: Pass the date as a prop or use a useEffect/useMemo to set it after mount.

issues.description: Main temperature display uses a hardcoded 64px font-size style override.
issues.fix: Use a design token for font-size or stick to the available 'size' props from the Text primitive.

evaluations.critique: The component is visually professional with excellent hierarchy and effective use of the design system's layout primitives. It fully implements the required forecast strip and current weather details, though it misses basic accessibility requirements for non-text content (emojis).

evaluation.critique: The component has excellent data presentation and generally strong layout with proper use of design tokens. However, there are minor issues: the emoji size uses a hardcoded '64px' instead of a token, some color fallbacks use hardcoded hex values (though with proper token names), and interactive polish is limited—no hover/focus states or transitions on interactive elements like the condition badge or forecast items.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Grid elements are non-semantic divs with onClick handlers, making them inaccessible to keyboard and screen reader users.
issues.fix: Replace the cell div with a button or add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and an onKeyDown handler for keyboard interaction.

issues.description: The 'filteredElements' useMemo hook is computed but never used, while the same logic is duplicated in the 'isHighlighted' function.
issues.fix: Remove the unused filteredElements variable and consider refactoring isHighlighted to use a memoized set of IDs for performance.

issues.description: The grid layout uses a hardcoded 900px min-width and overflow-x: auto, which works but lacks a visual indicator for scrollability on smaller viewports.
issues.fix: Add a subtle shadow or gradient mask to indicate the horizontal scroll area.

evaluations.critique: The component successfully implements the 18-column grid layout and maintains table structure during search by using opacity for filtering. The visual design is professional with clear category color-coding and nice selection effects, but the lack of keyboard navigation for the grid cells is a notable accessibility gap.

evaluation.critique: The component demonstrates solid use of design tokens and proper data rendering from props, but has notable layout issues: the grid uses a hardcoded 18-column layout with overflowX requiring a minWidth of 900px, making it non-responsive on smaller screens. The code is cut off mid-detail panel, preventing full evaluation of the interactive detail view. Polish could be enhanced with proper focus states on the search field and smoother transitions.
